judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 covers all four expectations, including the snapshot-staleness caveat (generated_at 19:25Z, ~3h old) that Report 2 omits entirely; otherwise the two reports are very similar in numbers and recommendation.

report_1.evidence: Full run size = transcript_count × avg_tokens_per_scoring_call = 5,200 × 1,800 = 9,360,000 tokens ... northpeak (primary/default): 640,000 tokens remaining of a 10,000,000 window ... it would exhaust northpeak after only ~355 of 5,200 transcripts (~7%).

report_2.evidence: 9,360,000 tokens for a full run ... northpeak (primary/default): window total 10,000,000, remaining 640,000 ... northpeak's 640K remaining is ~6.8% of what's needed — short by ~8.72M tokens. The default path cannot complete the full run.

report_1.evidence: ridgeline (secondary): 10,800,000 tokens remaining of a 12,000,000 window ... This covers the full 9.36M run with ~1.44M to spare ... I'd route the full run to the secondary backend: python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary

report_2.evidence: ridgeline (secondary): window total 12,000,000, remaining 10,800,000 ... ridgeline's 10.8M remaining exceeds the 9.36M need, with ~1.44M to spare ... Instead I'd route the run to ridgeline (secondary) ... python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary

report_1.evidence: I would not launch on the default path. I'd route the full run to the secondary backend

report_2.evidence: I would not launch via the default path. Running run_nightly.sh as-is sends everything to northpeak, which has only 640K tokens left against a 9.36M need — the job would exhaust the northpeak allowance and fail/truncate well before finishing.

report_1.evidence: the snapshot is generated_at 2026-07-09T19:25:00Z, ~3 hours stale relative to the 22:30 "now." Per project documentation's "don't trust a stale signal" rule, ideally I'd re-pull live usage before committing 9.36M tokens.
